payload.claim_text: Passenger revenues were unfavorable by $23 million (2%) for YTD March, reflecting lower paid ridership, which was adversely impacted by extreme snowfall in both late January and February.
payload.subject: passenger revenues

tool_args.label: NYCT and Commuter Rail ridership at 75.5% of 2019

payload.claim_text: In March 2026, the actual ridership was 75.5% of pre-COVID levels for NYCT and Commuter Rail.
payload.subject: NYCT and Commuter Rail paid ridership

payload.claim_text: In March 2026, the actual ridership was 79.4% of pre-COVID levels for NYCT Subway.
payload.subject: NYCT Subway paid ridership

tool_args.raw_text: Mar-26 Actual Results 59.1% Financial Plan Forecast 61.0%

payload.claim_text: On an MTA-Wide basis, there were 3,120 vacancies in March, representing a vacancy rate of (4%), which was primarily comprised of vacancies in operations and maintenance (83% of total vacancies).
payload.subject: MTA-wide vacancies

tool_args.label: TBTA $500 million CRZ Tolling Program Loan March 2026
tool_args.raw_text: $500 million Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority 2026 Loan...closed a $500 million CRZ Tolling Program (also known as the Central Business District Tolling Program) loan

payload.description: CRZ Tolling Program (Central Business District Tolling Program) loan closed March 26, 2026

payload.claim_text: Passenger revenues remain significantly below pre-pandemic levels: YTD March passenger revenues were down $343 million (24%) compared with YTD March 2019.
payload.subject: passenger revenues
